marketState: neutral, fairly-valued market pulled two ways - bearish storage surplus vs a bullish near-term heat spike
summary: Front-month NG ($2.908) is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917, spread -$0.009), so there is little pure valuation edge right now. The bearish anchor is storage: inventories sit +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and seasonality shows price running -11.3pp below its typical year with only a 31% historical chance of being higher in 4 weeks (typical move -3.1%), though that seasonal read is explicitly flagged low-reliability (50pp 10-90 spread). Partially offsetting the bearish tilt, near-term weather demand is running +25.5% above the 5-year norm and above the 5-year max (rangePos 1.08) over the next 7 days, even as the 8-16 day outlook cools back toward more normal levels (+18.6%, rangePos 0.87). Technicals are uniformly bearish (50<200 EMA cross and mostly negative MACD from 5m to 1w) yet stretched - RSI 22-30 on 1h/4h and price already below the lower daily Bollinger band - leaving short-term direction genuinely mixed. Managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts) but only at the 35th percentile of the past 5 years, so positioning is not extreme in either direction, and the futures curve carries a normal steep winter contango (Dec/Jan premium) with only slight front-month backwardation.

longTerm.entry: $2.93 - $2.98 (scale in on rallies toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale in, do not add below stop
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and seasonality shows price already below median with only 31% odds of a higher print in 4 weeks (typical -3.1% move) - both point the same bearish direction over this horizon, even though the fair-value band itself drifts up seasonally ($2.917 -> $3.071 by +21D).
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained cold shift in the 8-16D weather outlook, a run of bearish (smaller-than-forecast) storage builds, or price closing and holding above $3.15 would invalidate this thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather demand is extreme (+25.5% vs 5y, above the 5y max) which argues for upside, but every technical timeframe from 5m to 1w shows a bearish trend and death cross, and price is sitting almost exactly at fair value with no clean edge either way - a genuinely mixed setup not worth forcing a trade on.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for confirmation: either RSI(1h/4h) reclaiming above ~45 with a close back above $2.97 (plus7D fair value) to go LONG the heat-demand pop, or a daily close below $2.85 confirming trend continuation to go SHORT toward $2.70.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build
Extreme near-term heat demand (+25.5% vs 5y, above the 5y max) fading into the 8-16D window (+18.6%)
Seasonality below median (-11.3pp) with low reliability and only 31% odds of a higher price in 4 weeks
Price essentially at fair value across all horizons ($2.908 vs $2.917 now)
All technical timeframes bearish trend/death-cross but several are oversold (RSI 22-30)
risks: The heat-driven demand spike could force a sharp short-covering rally given widespread oversold technical readings
Low seasonality reliability (50pp 10-90 spread) means the bearish seasonal tilt could easily fail to play out
A colder shift in the 8-16D outlook or a supply disruption could quickly overturn the bearish storage-surplus narrative
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
